meta_title: For Freelancers: freelance pitching to Solve can't debug code (Intermediate Case Study)
meta_description: Learn how to freelance pitching and overcome can't debug code. A Intermediate-level Case Study using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: Freelance Pitching: Solving Debugging Bottlenecks Fast
content.seo_description: Stop struggling with impossible bugs. Learn how to leverage freelance pitching and AI tools like Antigravity and Claude Code to deliver faster, cleaner results.
content.answer_block: When you hit a debugging wall, stop burning billable hours. Pivot your workflow to an AI-augmented audit. By using Antigravity for architectural mapping and Claude Code for iterative repairs, you transform 'can't debug' into a high-value freelance pitch, positioning yourself as an efficient fixer rather than a struggling coder.
content.intro:
The screen glows at 3 AM. A critical bug has stalled your deployment, and the client’s deadline is looming like a guillotine. You are stuck in a cycle of trial-and-error, watching your profit margin evaporate with every failed console log. The frustration isn't just technical; it's the professional anxiety that you are losing your edge.

But what if you stopped fighting the code and started orchestrating it? The secret to scaling as a freelancer isn't knowing every syntax by heart—it’s mastering the art of the AI-augmented audit. By shifting your perspective from 'fixing bugs' to 'architecting solutions,' you turn technical debt into your most lucrative freelance pitch.

steps.title: Architectural Audit
steps.description: Before touching the code, map the dependency graph to find the root cause of the failure.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Analyze this project structure. Identify circular dependencies or unhandled edge cases that typically cause runtime crashes in this stack.

steps.title: Automated Bug Isolation
steps.description: Isolate the failing module by creating a minimal reproduction script.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a test harness for the module identified as the primary failure point. Include error handling for null pointers.

steps.title: Predictive Fix Implementation
steps.description: Use Claude Code to suggest a fix based on your architectural map.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Given the dependency graph, propose a refactor that fixes the runtime error without breaking dependent downstream services.

steps.title: Regression Testing
steps.description: Ensure the fix doesn't introduce new bugs in related modules.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Simulate a load test on the updated module and compare outcomes against the baseline performance metrics.

steps.title: Client Value Packaging
steps.description: Document the fix as a value-add report to include in your freelance pitch.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a summary of the technical debt resolved. Focus on long-term performance gains and security hardening for the client.

pro_tips: Never debug in isolation; always ask the AI to explain the 'why' behind the bug so you can explain it to the client later.
Use CLI history logs as 'proof of work' to show clients how much effort you saved them via automation.
Always set a 'maximum tokens' constraint when debugging to prevent the AI from hallucinating complex, non-working solutions.
Turn your debugging process into a 'Maintenance Retainer' pitch by showing the client the recurring patterns in their codebase.
Keep your Antigravity context files lean; only include the relevant module to prevent the AI from being overwhelmed by irrelevant codebase noise.
content.next_steps: Take your newly created audit report, package it into a PDF, and present it to your client as a 'System Health & Optimization' service to justify an increased hourly rate.
content.conversion_block: You are now equipped to handle any codebase, no matter how chaotic. Stop trading time for stress and start trading expertise for premium fees. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
